L1.area: Agent sandbox filesystem access and bridge layer

L2.description: A TOCTOU race condition exists where the sandbox path validation checks (canonical path resolution and hardlink detection) are performed as separate operations from the actual file read. An attacker can exploit the time window between the safety checks and the file access to swap the target via symlink manipulation, escaping the sandbox to read arbitrary files on the host filesystem.

L3.area: Remote shell sandbox filesystem bridge and its file reading logic
L3.description: A TOCTOU race condition exists in the remote shell sandbox where filesystem path validation (canonical path resolution and hardlink checks) runs as separate shell commands from the actual file read. An attacker who can manipulate the filesystem between these operations can swap the validated path target via symlink, escaping the sandbox to read arbitrary files on the host. The vulnerability specifically affects remote sandbox file access over shell execution, not the local filesystem bridge.

ground_truth.reason: The readFile method performs path canonicalization and hardlink checks as separate shell commands before reading via cat, creating a TOCTOU window where an attacker can swap a symlink to escape the sandbox and read arbitrary host files.
